sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The bars are growing and now they're getting closer to the purple L-shape. I can see the yellow bar is now touching the purple L-shape's vertical arm. Let me check the current bar sizes and see if they're approaching the L-shape's dimensions.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current bar sizes
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Colored bars:")
total_bar_px = 0
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['O', 'S', 'Y', 'N']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        h = max(rows) - min(rows) + 1
        w = max(cols) - min(cols) + 1
        total_bar_px += n['pixels']
        print(f"  {n['color']}: {n['pixels']}px, rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)} (h={h}), cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)} (w={w})")

print(f"\nTotal bar pixels: {total_bar_px}")

print("\nPurple L-shape:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'p':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        h = max(rows) - min(rows) + 1
        w = max(cols) - min(cols) + 1
        print(f"  {n['pixels']}px, rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)} (h={h}), cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)} (w={w})")

print("\nRed dot:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'r' and n['pixels'] == 1:
        b = n['boundary']
        print(f"  at ({b[0][0]}, {b[0][1]})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
